judgmentZh: 损：有孚，元吉，无咎，可贞，利有攸往。曷之用，二簋可用享。
judgmentEn: Decrease combined with sincerity brings about supreme good fortune without blame. One may be persevering in this. It furthers one to undertake something. How is this to be carried out? One may use two small bowls for the sacrifice.
imageZh: 山下有泽，损；君子以惩忿窒欲。
imageEn: At the foot of the mountain, the lake: the image of Decrease. Thus the superior man controls his anger and restrains his instincts.
interpretationZh: 损卦象征减损，山下有泽，泽水蒸发润泽山林。减损自己以利他人，诚心则大吉。损下益上，克制私欲，修养德行。
interpretationEn: Sun symbolizes decrease and sacrifice. The lake at the foot of the mountain evaporates to nourish it. Sincere self-sacrifice for the greater good brings supreme fortune.

lines.textZh: 初九：已事遄往，无咎，酌损之。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Going quickly when one's tasks are finished is without blame. But one must reflect on how much one may decrease others.
lines.interpretationZh: 办完自己的事赶快前去帮助，无过失，但要斟酌减损的程度。
lines.interpretationEn: Finish your own tasks quickly and go to help. Consider how much to give.
lines.detailZh: “已事遄往，无咎，酌损之。”初九处损之始，阳刚而能行，说明事情一旦该处理，就应迅速前往，不可拖延。但其关键在“酌损”，即损减要有分寸，不可过猛。此爻强调在局势初动时，及时调整、适度退让，反而无咎。
lines.detailEn: “When affairs are completed, go quickly. No blame. Consider carefully what should be decreased.” At the beginning of Decrease, this line shows energetic action joined with moderation. Once something must be handled, hesitation is harmful. Yet the reduction must be measured, not excessive. Timely and proportionate adjustment prevents blame.

lines.textZh: 九二：利贞，征凶，弗损益之。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: Perseverance furthers. To undertake something brings misfortune. Without decreasing oneself, one is able to bring increase to others.
lines.interpretationZh: 宜坚守正道，冒进则凶。不减损自己也能使他人受益。
lines.interpretationEn: Persevere in your position. Rash action brings misfortune. One can benefit others without diminishing oneself.
lines.detailZh: “利贞，征凶，弗损益之。”九二居中得正，本可守持中道，因此利于正固；若主动进征、强行作为，反而有凶。这里的“弗损益之”不是不要变化，而是说不必妄自减损，守中即是增益。此爻提醒人在条件尚稳时，不可为求表现而多动。
lines.detailEn: “Perseverance is favorable. To advance brings misfortune. Do not decrease it; this will increase it.” Firmly centered, this line advises stability over aggressive advance. Forced movement creates trouble. Sometimes the best increase comes from not diminishing oneself unnecessarily. Hold the center, and quiet strength grows on its own.

lines.textZh: 六三：三人行则损一人，一人行则得其友。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: When three people journey together, their number decreases by one. When one man journeys alone, he finds a companion.
lines.interpretationZh: 三人同行必减一人，一人独行则得良友。事物需要配合得当。
lines.interpretationEn: Three travelers lose one companion. A lone traveler finds a friend. Proper pairing is essential.
lines.detailZh: “三人行则损一人，一人行则得其友。”六三言关系中的取舍法则。人多则意见杂、牵制多，必有一人被减去；一人独行，反而可能遇到真正同道者。此爻说明损有时体现为社交与合作的筛选，去掉不合拍、不必要的牵连，方能获得真正的支持。
lines.detailEn: “If three people travel together, one will be lost. If one goes alone, one finds a companion.” This line concerns selection in relationships and alliances. Too many participants bring friction and dilution. By reducing unnecessary company, one may find a true partner. Decrease here means refining associations so authentic cooperation can emerge.

lines.textZh: 六四：损其疾，使遄有喜，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: If a man decreases his faults, it makes the other hasten to come and rejoice. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 减损自己的毛病，使人欢喜前来，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Decrease your faults and others will joyfully come to you. No blame.
lines.detailZh: “损其疾，使遄有喜，无咎。”六四之损，不是损其正，而是损其疾，亦即去除弊病、偏失、积患。能够迅速除病，便会很快有喜。这一爻最能体现损卦的积极意义：减少问题本身，就是最大的收获。现实中常指戒除坏习惯、纠正错误、及时止损。
lines.detailEn: “Decrease his illness. Make haste and there will be joy. No blame.” This line shows the most constructive form of decrease: removing what is diseased or harmful. What is reduced is not one’s true value, but one’s trouble. Quick correction brings relief and happiness. It often points to cutting off bad habits, errors, or ongoing damage.

lines.textZh: 六五：或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，元吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Someone does indeed increase him. Ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 有人赠予价值十朋的神龟，不可违拒，大吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Someone bestows increase. Even ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose this. Supreme good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，元吉。”六五柔中居尊，能以谦受益，因此会得到极大帮助，连最珍贵的宝龟都有人奉上，无法推辞。此爻说明能损己者终得人助，居上而不自满，反能汇聚资源与信任，因此大吉。
lines.detailEn: “Someone indeed increases him with tortoise shells worth ten pairs of friends. One cannot refuse them. Supreme good fortune.” Occupying a central and receptive place, this line shows that one who is humble and self-restrained attracts real support. Because ego is reduced, help arrives freely and abundantly. This is the paradox of Decrease: sincere self-limitation invites greater blessing.

lines.textZh: 上九：弗损益之，无咎，贞吉，利有攸往，得臣无家。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: If one is increased without depriving others, there is no blame. Perseverance brings good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something. One obtains servants but no longer has a separate home.
lines.interpretationZh: 不减损别人而增益自己，无咎，坚守正道则吉，利于有所作为。
lines.interpretationEn: Increase without decreasing others. No blame. Perseverance brings fortune. One gains followers who share a common purpose.
lines.detailZh: “弗损益之，无咎，贞吉，利有攸往，得臣无家。”上九居损之终，境界已高，不再是单向减损，而是超越小我，以不私其有而成全众人，所以“弗损益之”。能如此则无咎而吉，前往有利。“得臣无家”意谓因公忘私，所获助力不为一家一己，而为更大共同体所用。
lines.detailEn: “Do not decrease, but increase this. No blame. Perseverance brings good fortune. It is favorable to have somewhere to go. One gains servants but has no separate home.” At the top of the hexagram, decrease reaches transformation. The person is no longer preoccupied with personal loss, but serves a larger purpose. By transcending selfish possession, one benefits others and advances without blame.

overviewZh: 第四十一卦为损卦，上艮下兑，山高而泽低，泽气上升以润山，呈现出减下益上的结构，因此名为“损”。但《易》中的损，并非单纯失去，而是主动节制、适度减省，以成就更高秩序与更大利益。其核心精神在于：以诚信为本，以克己为功，以小损成大益。损不是匮乏，而是去除过度、裁抑私欲、简化浮华，使人回归真实与正道。此卦强调凡事不宜贪多，不可躁进，应懂得放下局部执念，舍弃无谓消耗，才能使局势趋于吉顺。对个人而言，损卦提示修身当先，从节欲、戒怒、减负开始；对组织而言，则意味着优化资源配置、收缩冗余、回归核心。真正的损，是为长远利益而进行的清醒取舍。
overviewEn: Hexagram 41, Decrease, consists of Mountain above and Lake below. The image suggests the lake giving of itself to nourish the mountain, forming the idea of reducing one part to benefit another. In the Book of Changes, decrease is not mere loss or deprivation. It means conscious restraint, simplification, and the willingness to let go of excess so that a healthier balance can emerge. Its wisdom lies in sacrificing what is secondary in order to preserve what is essential. This hexagram teaches that sincerity, moderation, and self-discipline turn apparent reduction into real blessing. One must not cling to every possession, emotion, or ambition. Instead, by reducing greed, anger, vanity, and waste, one creates space for clarity and steady progress. On a personal level, Decrease points to cultivation through self-restraint and inner refinement. In practical affairs, it suggests trimming excess, reallocating resources, and focusing on core priorities. True decrease is not collapse, but an intentional adjustment that leads to renewal, trust, and durable good fortune.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“损：有孚，元吉，无咎，可贞，利有攸往。曷之用，二簋可用享。”所谓“有孚”，说明损之道必须建立在真诚与信任之上，若出于勉强、虚饰或算计，则不能成吉。“元吉，无咎”，指出只要损得其正，便非但无害，反而大善。《彖传》说：“损，损下益上，其道上行。损而有孚，元吉，无咎，可贞，利有攸往。曷之用？二簋可用享；二簋应有时。损刚益柔有时，损益盈虚，与时偕行。”这说明损并不是绝对原则，而是顺时调节的法则。祭祀不在奢丰，二簋薄礼亦可致诚，强调形式可简，诚意不可减。真正的损，是因时制宜地减去刚强、浮盈、奢侈与执拗，使整体重新平衡。因此此卦既讲节制，也讲时机；既讲舍弃，也讲成全。守正而损，则能由减入增，由退成进。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Decrease. There is sincerity. Supreme good fortune. No blame. Perseverance is favorable. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. How may this be carried out? Even two small bowls may be used for the offering.” The key phrase is sincerity. Decrease becomes auspicious only when it is rooted in honesty rather than compulsion, display, or hidden calculation. The Tuanzhuan explains: “Decrease means reducing below to benefit above; its way moves upward. Decrease with sincerity brings supreme good fortune, no blame, and favorable perseverance. Even two bowls may be used in sacrifice, for simplicity has its proper time. Decreasing the firm and increasing the yielding occurs according to time; decrease and increase, fullness and emptiness, go forward with the times.” This commentary makes clear that decrease is not blind austerity. It is a timely rebalancing. Outer forms may be simplified, yet inner devotion must remain whole. What should be reduced are excess, rigidity, pride, and waste. What should be preserved are truth, order, and purpose. Thus the hexagram teaches measured sacrifice, not self-harm: by letting go at the right time and in the right spirit, one creates conditions for genuine advance and lasting benefit.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“山下有泽，损；君子以惩忿窒欲。”泽在山下，本有润上之势，象征下有所减，而上有所成。山因此更稳，泽因此不泛，形成一种克制中见成全的格局。君子观此卦象，明白人生许多混乱并非来自外界匮乏，而来自内在情绪与欲望失衡，所以修身的关键，在于“惩忿窒欲”。惩忿，是抑制暴躁、冲动、好胜之气；窒欲，是收束无穷扩张的私欲和贪念。当忿怒与欲望减少，判断便更清明，行为也更合乎分寸。损卦之象因此不是消极压抑，而是积极整饬：去其过满，守其中和，使人格与处境都重归稳定。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “At the foot of the mountain is the lake: the image of Decrease. Thus the superior person restrains anger and curbs desire.” The lake beneath the mountain suggests that what is below gives upward, making the mountain more stable while preventing the lake from overflowing. This is not destruction, but disciplined reallocation. From this image, the noble person learns that many human troubles come less from external shortage than from internal excess. Therefore the work of self-cultivation lies in restraining anger and limiting desire. To restrain anger is to check impulsiveness, aggression, and reactive pride. To curb desire is to reduce restless craving, vanity, and endless appetite. When these are moderated, one’s judgment becomes clearer and action becomes proportionate. The image of Decrease is therefore not bleak repression, but constructive refinement. By removing excess emotion and appetite, a person restores balance, dignity, and inner strength.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代生活中，损卦常被理解为一种“主动减法”的智慧。事业上，它提示不宜盲目扩张、铺摊子过大，必要时应砍掉低效项目、收拢战线，把资源集中到最核心的方向，短期似损，长期反而增益。感情上，损卦强调减少控制欲、占有欲与情绪消耗，少一些自我中心，多一些体谅和真诚，关系反而更稳固。财运上，此卦不利奢侈挥霍，却利于节流、储蓄、债务整理及理性配置资产，特别适合在风险上升阶段做保守调整。健康上，损卦提醒人减去过量饮食、熬夜、焦虑、酒色与情绪内耗，身体的修复往往不是靠“再加什么”，而是先停止无谓透支。总体而言，损卦适合用于整理人生结构：删繁就简、收心敛欲、以退为进。真正高明的人，不是永远做加法，而是知道什么时候该减，减什么，减到何种程度，才能让生活重新恢复秩序与生机。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 41 speaks through the wisdom of deliberate subtraction. In career matters, it advises against reckless expansion and scattered commitments. When necessary, one should cut inefficient projects, simplify operations, and concentrate effort on what is truly strategic. What looks like short-term loss may become long-term gain. In relationships, Decrease suggests reducing control, possessiveness, and emotional overreaction. Less ego and less demand often create more trust, ease, and genuine closeness. In financial matters, this hexagram discourages luxury, waste, and impulsive spending, while favoring savings, debt reduction, budget discipline, and cautious asset allocation, especially in unstable times. In health, it points toward removing what drains vitality: overeating, lack of sleep, excess stimulation, addictive habits, and chronic stress. Recovery often begins not by adding more remedies, but by stopping unnecessary depletion. Overall, Decrease is a guide to restructuring life through simplicity and restraint. The mature person does not merely seek more; they know what to release, when to scale back, and how much to sacrifice so that balance, strength, and lasting benefit can return.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周文王治国之道来会通损卦之义。文王在积德兴周之时，并不以暴烈争胜，而是先修内政、薄赋敛、重农桑、施仁德，以减轻百姓负担来培植国家根基。这种做法表面看是“损上之欲”，不事奢靡与苛取，实则是“益国之本”。损卦所说的“二簋可用享”，也正合古人重诚不重奢的政治伦理：祭祀与治理皆贵在诚敬，不在浮华。文王能以克制代替放纵，以节用代替滥费，以长远代替急功，因此终使民心归附，周室日强。此故事说明，真正高明的领导者，往往懂得先损自己的欲望与排场，再成就更大的事业。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical parallel for Hexagram 41 can be seen in the governing style attributed to King Wen of Zhou. Rather than seeking dominance through harsh force or extravagant display, he strengthened the state by cultivating virtue, lightening burdens, supporting agriculture, and practicing benevolence. On the surface, this meant reducing the ruler’s appetite for luxury, taxation, and immediate extraction. In substance, it increased the true foundation of the realm. This reflects the hexagram’s line about simple offerings: sincerity matters more than excess. In both ritual and government, authenticity outweighs splendor. By restraining indulgence, conserving resources, and thinking beyond short-term gain, King Wen won trust and gradually built lasting power. The story illustrates a central lesson of Decrease: wise leadership often begins with self-limitation. One gives up display and personal appetite so that a deeper, broader form of prosperity can emerge.
relatedHexagramsNote: 损与第四十二卦“益”互为综卦，一减一增，相反而相成。损教人节制、舍弃、调亏补偏；益教人滋养、扶助、扩大善果。二者都强调“与时偕行”，不是固定追求增加或减少，而是依据时势决定何时该收、何时该放。懂损，方能真益。

judgmentEn: Increase. It furthers one to undertake something. It furthers one to cross the great water.
imageZh: 风雷，益；君子以见善则迁，有过则改。
imageEn: Wind and thunder: the image of Increase. Thus the superior man, if he sees good, imitates it; if he has faults, he rids himself of them.
interpretationZh: 益卦象征增益，风雷激荡，相互助长。损上益下，利于行动和冒险。见贤思齐，有过即改。
interpretationEn: Yi symbolizes increase. Wind and thunder strengthen each other. The upper decreases to benefit the lower. A time for action, improvement, and crossing obstacles.

lines.textZh: 初九：利用为大作，元吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 利于做大事，大吉，无过失。得到上方的增益支持。
lines.interpretationEn: It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune and no blame.
lines.detailZh: 初九曰“利用为大作，元吉，无咎”。居下而当动之始，阳刚有为，象征在增益初期应把握时机，投入正大事业。所谓“大作”，不是盲目扩张，而是趁势建立根基、开创新局。只要所作符合正道，虽任务艰巨，终能大吉而无咎。
lines.detailEn: The first line says, “It is beneficial to undertake great works. Supreme good fortune. No blame.” At the beginning of movement, this yang line represents initiative at the proper moment. In the early stage of increase, one should commit energy to worthy and substantial undertakings. “Great works” does not mean reckless expansion, but building foundations and opening a new situation in alignment with what is right. When the effort is upright, even major tasks bring good fortune without blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，永贞吉。王用享于帝，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Someone does indeed increase him; ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Constant perseverance brings good fortune. The king presents him before God. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 有人赠予十朋之龟，不可违拒，永远坚守正道则吉。君王祭祀上帝，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Someone bestows great increase. Ten pairs of tortoises cannot oppose it. Eternal perseverance brings fortune. The king makes offerings to God.
lines.detailZh: 六二曰“或益之十朋之龟，弗克违，永贞吉。王用享于帝，吉。”此爻柔顺中正，得外来之助，连贵重之龟都象征天命所归，不可违逆。它提示人在受益时要守正持久，不可因好运而轻浮。若能怀敬慎之心，如王者祭帝般诚敬，则吉上加吉。
lines.detailEn: The second line states, “One may be increased by the value of ten pairs of tortoise shells. One cannot oppose it. Perseverance brings good fortune. The king uses this to offer sacrifice to the Supreme Deity—good fortune.” This yielding yet central line suggests receiving significant help or blessing from outside. The precious tortoise shells symbolize value and even heavenly confirmation. The lesson is that when benefit comes, one must remain steady and correct rather than becoming careless. With reverence and sincerity, good fortune is deepened.

lines.textZh: 六三：益之用凶事，无咎。有孚中行，告公用圭。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One is enriched through unfortunate events. No blame, if you are sincere and walk in the middle, and report to the prince with a seal.
lines.interpretationZh: 因凶事而获益，无过失。有诚信行中道，以玉圭禀告公侯。
lines.interpretationEn: Increase comes through misfortune. No blame if sincere and following the middle way. Report to authority with proper ceremony.
lines.detailZh: 六三曰“益之用凶事，无咎。有孚中行，告公用圭。”此爻处下卦之上，接近险变，说明增益有时并不体现在顺境享受，而在应对危难时得到支援。若遇凶事，能以诚信守中，如实禀告上位，并持信物而行，则虽在困难中，仍可无咎。它强调危机中的诚实与程序正当。
lines.detailEn: The third line says, “Increase is used in dealing with unfortunate affairs. No blame. With trust, one walks the middle course and informs the ruler, using a jade tablet.” Positioned near transition, this line shows that increase does not always appear as comfort; sometimes it comes as support amid crisis. In difficult circumstances, if one remains sincere, balanced, and properly reports matters to authority, blame can be avoided. The line emphasizes honesty, moderation, and proper conduct during hardship.

lines.textZh: 六四：中行，告公从。利用为依迁国。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: If you walk in the middle and report to the prince, he will follow. It furthers one to be used in the removal of the capital.
lines.interpretationZh: 行中道禀告公侯，公侯听从。利于作为依靠迁移国都。
lines.interpretationEn: Walking the middle way and reporting to the prince, he follows. It furthers one to assist in moving the capital.
lines.detailZh: 六四曰“中行，告公从。利用为依迁国。”六四近君而柔顺，能承上启下。所谓“迁国”象征重大调整，包括迁都、迁制、迁策，意味着在关键变局中，只要持中而行，取得上层信任，就有利于完成依附大局的迁移与改革。此爻特别利于组织重组、战略转型。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line reads, “Walking in the middle, one informs the ruler and is followed. It is beneficial to use this as support for moving the state.” Close to authority yet receptive, this line bridges upper and lower levels. “Moving the state” symbolizes major reorganization—relocation, reform, or strategic transition. If one proceeds with balance and gains the trust of leadership, large-scale change can be carried through successfully. It especially favors institutional adjustment and strategic transformation.

lines.textZh: 九五：有孚惠心，勿问元吉。有孚惠我德。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If in truth you have a kind heart, ask not. Supreme good fortune. Truly, kindness will be recognized as your virtue.
lines.interpretationZh: 有诚信和仁爱之心，不必问，大吉。诚信仁爱就是我的德行。
lines.interpretationEn: With a sincere and kind heart, do not ask — supreme good fortune. Your kindness is recognized as true virtue.
lines.detailZh: 九五曰“有孚惠心，勿问元吉。有孚惠我德。”九五居尊位而中正，是全卦最美的一爻。真正的领导者以诚信与恩惠待人，不必反复计较得失，自有大吉。其德行会反过来感化众人，使人心归附。此爻说明最大的“益”，不是财货，而是以德施惠、以诚聚众。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says, “With trust and a benevolent heart, do not question it—supreme good fortune. With trust, others benefit from my virtue.” In the honored central position, this is the most elevated line of the hexagram. A true leader benefits others through sincerity and kindness, without anxious calculation over immediate returns. Such virtue naturally inspires loyalty and response. The deepest form of increase, this line teaches, is not wealth alone but the power of trustworthy generosity and moral influence.

lines.textZh: 上九：莫益之，或击之，立心勿恒，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He brings increase to no one. Indeed, someone even strikes him. He does not keep his heart constantly steady. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有人增益他，反而有人攻击他。心志不坚定，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: No one increases him; indeed he is attacked. An inconstant heart brings misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 上九曰“莫益之，或击之，立心勿恒，凶。”益至上极而反，若居高位仍只求他人继续加益于己，便会失道。既得不到帮助，反而可能招来攻击。其根源在“立心勿恒”——初心不定，德行不守，欲望失衡。此爻警告：增长到顶点时更需反省节制，否则盛极而衰。
lines.detailEn: The top line states, “No one increases him; someone may even strike him. If one’s heart is not constant, misfortune.” When increase reaches its extreme, reversal becomes possible. If a person in a high position still seeks benefit only for himself, he loses the Way. Help no longer comes, and opposition may arise instead. The root problem is an inconstant heart—unstable intention, weak virtue, and unrestrained desire. This line warns that at the height of growth, self-restraint and reflection are most necessary, or prosperity will turn toward decline.

overviewZh: 益卦，上巽下震，风行雷动，象征在行动中注入助力，在生发中形成增长。它所说的“益”，不是单纯的物质增多，而是顺势施惠、损上益下、使整体生命力被激活的过程。卦辞言“利有攸往，利涉大川”，说明当局势处于可增长阶段时，不宜保守停滞，而应主动前行，敢于承担较大的转变与挑战。益之道贵在流通，贵在把资源、德行、机会投向真正需要之处，从而形成正向循环。《象》曰“见善则迁，有过则改”，点出此卦的内核并非一味索取，而是通过学习、修正、自我更新，令个人与群体共同受益。因此，益卦既是增长之卦，也是修德之卦，强调真正长久的收益，来自诚意、行动与利他的结合。
overviewEn: Hexagram 42, Increase, is formed by Wind above Thunder. Wind spreads, Thunder stirs; together they portray growth that emerges through movement, encouragement, and the circulation of energy. “Increase” here does not merely mean gaining more possessions. It refers to a living process in which support is directed where it is needed, vitality is awakened, and the whole situation becomes more flourishing. The Judgment says, “It is beneficial to have somewhere to go; it is beneficial to cross the great river,” indicating that when the time for growth arrives, one should not remain passive or defensive. Progress, expansion, and even bold transitions become favorable. Yet this hexagram is not about greed. Its deeper teaching lies in channeling benefit wisely, sharing resources, and aligning action with sincerity and moral clarity. The Image adds that the noble person, on seeing good, changes accordingly, and on having faults, corrects them. Thus Increase is as much about inner improvement as outer gain: true increase comes when generosity, timely action, and self-renewal reinforce one another.
judgmentDetailZh: 《彖传》说：“益，损上益下，民说无疆；自上下下，其道大光。利有攸往，中正有庆；利涉大川，木道乃行。益动而巽，日进无疆。”这几句把益卦的精神讲得极透。所谓益，不是强者继续堆积，而是能把高位者的资源、责任与恩泽，向下施与，使众人受益，因此民心欢悦，事业也更有光明。下体震为动，上体巽为入，表示行动而能顺入人心，推进而不刚暴，所以适合有所前往。又言“木道乃行”，古人舟楫渡川，多取木之象，说明在正确时机与正当方法下，即使是涉险之举，也可通达。益卦最重要的判断标准，是是否合乎“中正”与“有孚”：增加若出于私欲，终将反噬；增加若出于公心与诚信，则能使人我两利，福庆不断。
judgmentDetailEn: The Tuan Commentary explains: “Increase means diminishing what is above to augment what is below; the people rejoice without limit. From above descending to below, its way shines greatly. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go, for central correctness brings blessing. It is beneficial to cross the great river, for the way of wood can proceed. Increase moves and is gentle, therefore its daily advance knows no bounds.” This passage reveals the heart of the hexagram. True increase is not the powerful accumulating ever more for themselves. Rather, it is the conscious transfer of resources, responsibility, and support from higher places to where growth is needed. That is why the people rejoice and why the way becomes bright. Thunder below signifies movement; Wind above signifies penetration and gentle influence. Thus progress succeeds not through force alone but through action that enters hearts and circumstances appropriately. The line about crossing the great river suggests that even difficult undertakings can succeed when timing, means, and moral alignment are right. The key standard in this hexagram is centrality, correctness, and trustworthiness. Increase driven by selfish appetite becomes unstable; increase guided by sincerity and public-mindedness creates enduring blessing for both self and others.
imageDetailZh: 益卦之象为“风雷，益”。雷在下而动，风在上而行，仿佛雷发于地，风随而布，万物因而振起、舒展、增长。雷代表发动、生机、决断，风代表传播、教化、渗透、影响；二者相合，说明真正的增益并不是静态囤积，而是让善意与能量流动起来。故《象》曰：“君子以见善则迁，有过则改。”见善则迁，是懂得吸纳外界优点，不固守旧见；有过则改，是敢于面对缺陷，及时修正偏差。由此可见，益的根本不在外财，而在内在更新。一个人若能不断向善而变、因错而改，他的德行、关系、事业自然都会增长。
imageDetailEn: The Image of Hexagram 42 is “Wind and Thunder: Increase.” Thunder rises from below as a force of awakening, while Wind moves above as a power of diffusion and influence. Together they suggest life quickening, spreading, and growing. Thunder represents initiative, vitality, and decisive activation; Wind represents penetration, teaching, persuasion, and subtle reach. Their union shows that real increase is not stagnant accumulation but the circulation of good influence and living energy. Therefore the Image says, “The noble person, on seeing good, moves toward it; on having faults, corrects them.” To move toward the good means to learn without stubbornness and to adapt when a better way appears. To correct faults means facing one’s deficiencies honestly and changing course in time. This reveals the core of Increase: outward gain is secondary, while inward renewal is fundamental. When a person continually reforms, learns, and aligns with what is better, growth naturally extends into character, relationships, and work.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：益卦在事业上主利于拓展、合作、资源倾斜与制度优化。适合争取支持、推进项目、打开新市场，但前提是方向正当、团队共享成果，不能只肥一端。感情：此卦提示关系中的“增益”来自真诚付出与及时修正。伴侣之间若愿意看见对方优点、也肯承认自身问题，感情会明显升温；若只想索取回报，则易由益转损。财运：财务上属于可增之象，但重在合理流动，不宜死守。投资、合作、学习型投入较有利，尤其适合把钱用在提升能力、改善结构、帮助关键节点上。健康：益卦强调生机提升，适合建立新的健康习惯，如规律作息、循序运动、调整饮食。若身体已有小问题，应“有过则改”，尽早检查与纠正。整体而言，益卦提醒现代人：增长不是侥幸暴富，而是通过利他、修正、行动与持续改善，让自己进入更健康的上升循环。
modernApplicationEn: Career: In professional life, Increase favors expansion, collaboration, resource allocation, and structural improvement. It is a good time to seek support, advance important projects, and open new markets, provided the direction is sound and the gains are shared rather than concentrated selfishly. Relationships: In love and partnership, this hexagram teaches that increase comes through sincere giving and timely correction. When both people recognize each other’s strengths and admit their own faults, warmth and trust grow quickly. If one side only wants returns, increase turns into depletion. Wealth: Financially, this is a sign of potential growth, but growth must remain in healthy circulation rather than hoarding. Investment in learning, strategic cooperation, and long-term capability building is especially favorable. Money used to strengthen key foundations is more auspicious than speculative greed. Health: Increase points to renewed vitality and the benefit of building better habits—regular sleep, gradual exercise, improved diet, and sustained self-care. If a small imbalance already exists, correction should happen early rather than being delayed. Overall, this hexagram reminds modern people that real increase is not sudden luck alone. It arises from generosity, reform, wise action, and a sustained upward cycle of improvement.
historicalStoryZh: 论益卦的历史意涵，最容易联想到大禹治水。洪患当前，若只顾保存局部利益，终将两败俱伤；禹则奔走四方，疏导江河，因地制宜，把力量用在拯救整体生民之上。这正符合“损上益下”的精神：上位者承担更多劳苦，把利益让渡给天下，最终换来的是万民安定与王道光明。治水过程中又须“利涉大川”，亲历险阻，持续行动，而非空谈。禹并非一时施恩，而是在长期实践中不断修正旧法，改堵为疏，这也契合“见善则迁，有过则改”。因此，益卦不只是受益之象，更是治理、担当与自我革新的象征。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical illustration of Hexagram 42 is Yu the Great controlling the floods. Faced with widespread disaster, he did not protect narrow interests or seek comfort in high position. Instead, he traveled across the land, studied the terrain, opened channels, and guided the waters so that the people as a whole could survive and prosper. This reflects the spirit of “diminishing what is above to benefit what is below”: those in higher places bear more burden and direct benefit downward, thereby bringing broad stability and lasting legitimacy. The work also embodies “beneficial to cross the great river,” for it required entering danger directly and acting continuously rather than speaking in abstractions. Yu’s greatness lay not only in effort but in correction: he departed from ineffective blockage methods and adopted dredging and guidance instead. In that sense, Increase is not merely about receiving advantage; it is about responsible leadership, practical service, and the willingness to reform one’s methods for the good of all.
relatedHexagramsNote: 益与损为相对之卦：损讲减损私欲、节制有度，益讲将资源导向更有生机之处，形成增长。又可参看风雷类象中的恒卦，恒重持久，益重增进；若无恒，益难久；若无益，恒易滞。懂得先损后益、以正致益，方为长久之道。
references: 《彖传》：益，损上益下，民说无疆；自上下下，其道大光。
《象传》：风雷，益；君子以见善则迁，有过则改。

judgmentZh: 夬：扬于王庭，孚号，有厉，告自邑，不利即戎，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Breakthrough. One must resolutely make the matter known at the court of the king. It must be announced truthfully. Danger. It is necessary to notify one's own city. It does not further to resort to arms. It furthers one to undertake something.
imageZh: 泽上于天，夬；君子以施禄及下，居德则忌。
imageEn: The lake has risen up to heaven: the image of Breakthrough. Thus the superior man dispenses riches downward and refrains from resting on his virtue.
interpretationZh: 夬卦象征决断突破，泽水升于天上，终将倾泻而下。五阳决一阴，以正除邪，但须光明正大，不可诉诸武力。
interpretationEn: Guai symbolizes breakthrough and resolution. The lake rises to heaven and must overflow. Five yang lines resolve one yin. Act openly and justly, not by force.

lines.textZh: 初九：壮于前趾，往不胜为咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Mighty in the forward-striding toes. When one goes and is not equal to the undertaking, one makes a mistake.
lines.interpretationZh: 脚趾强壮急于前行，若力不能胜则有过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Mighty in the forward-striding toes. Going forward without adequate strength leads to blame.
lines.detailZh: ‘壮于前趾，往不胜为咎。’初九阳刚在下，志气先发，如人欲以前趾猛进。此爻告诫：虽有正气，但时位未充，若只凭一腔血勇仓促前冲，往往力有不逮，反而致咎。适合先蓄势、观势，而非逞强。
lines.detailEn: ‘Power in the toes. Advancing without victory brings blame.’ The first line shows eagerness at the very beginning. Strength is present, but position and timing are not yet sufficient. If one rushes ahead on courage alone, failure is likely. The lesson is to restrain premature action and build momentum before moving.

lines.textZh: 九二：惕号，莫夜有戎，勿恤。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A cry of alarm. Arms at evening and at night. Fear nothing.
lines.interpretationZh: 警惕呼号，夜间有兵戎之事，但不必忧虑。
lines.interpretationEn: A cry of alarm. Arms in the evening and night. Do not fear.
lines.detailZh: ‘惕号，莫夜有戎，勿恤。’九二居中得正，能够在危机中保持警觉。即使夜里似有兵戎骚动，也不必过度惊惧，因为已有防备。此爻强调决断之前的警戒系统：审慎、预案、戒备，能使危机不致成灾。
lines.detailEn: ‘Alert cry. In the night there are weapons. Do not worry.’ This line occupies a balanced place and responds to danger with vigilance rather than panic. It teaches preparedness: if one has already recognized the threat and taken precautions, even alarming signs at night need not lead to fear.

lines.textZh: 九三：壮于頄，有凶。君子夬夬，独行遇雨，若濡有愠，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: To be powerful in the cheekbones brings misfortune. The superior man is firmly resolved. He walks alone and is caught in the rain. He is bespattered, and people murmur against him. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 刚强表现在面颊上，有凶险。君子果断决行，独行遇雨被淋湿遭人怨怒，但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Showing strength in the face brings misfortune. The resolute man walks alone in the rain, bespattered and resented, but bears no blame.
lines.detailZh: ‘壮于頄，有凶。君子夬夬，独行遇雨，若濡有愠，无咎。’九三过刚，容易把决断变成逞强，故有凶象。但君子若能坚持原则、独自前行，即使遭遇误解、掣肘与狼狈，如遇雨而身湿、心有不悦，终究无咎。关键在于不因外界压力而失其正。
lines.detailEn: ‘Power in the cheekbones—misfortune. The noble one makes a resolute decision; walking alone, meeting rain, seeming wet and irritated, yet without blame.’ This line warns against aggressive hardness and confrontational ego. Still, if one remains true to principle despite isolation, misunderstanding, and discomfort, no blame results.

lines.textZh: 九四：臀无肤，其行次且。牵羊悔亡，闻言不信。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If a man were to let himself be led like a sheep, remorse would disappear. But if these words are heard, they would not be believed.
lines.interpretationZh: 臀部没有皮肤，行走艰难。若能像羊一样被牵引则悔恨消失，但听到这话却不肯相信。
lines.interpretationEn: No skin on the thighs; walking is painful. Let yourself be led like a sheep and remorse vanishes. But these words are not believed.
lines.detailZh: ‘臀无肤，其行次且。牵羊悔亡，闻言不信。’九四处近君位，却失于安稳，如臀无肤、行路艰难，表示推进受阻、心志不定。‘牵羊’喻顺势而行、借柔制刚；若肯听劝并调整方法，悔可亡。问题在于往往固执自用，虽闻忠言却未必真信。
lines.detailEn: ‘The buttocks have no skin; walking is difficult. If one leads a sheep, regret vanishes, but hearing words, one does not believe them.’ This line portrays discomfort, resistance, and awkward progress. The image of leading a sheep suggests yielding to the proper pull of circumstances. Regret can be removed if advice is accepted, but stubborn disbelief remains the main obstacle.

lines.textZh: 九五：苋陆夬夬，中行无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In dealing with weeds, firm resolution is necessary. Walking in the middle remains free of blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 像拔除苋陆杂草一样果断决断，行中道则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Firm resolution in uprooting weeds. Walking the middle path remains free of blame.
lines.detailZh: ‘苋陆夬夬，中行无咎。’九五居尊位，是全卦决断的主爻。苋陆柔蔓易除，象征虽有残余阴邪，但只要持中而行，处理得当，便可无咎。此爻强调领导者在关键时刻既要果断，也要中正，不可因胜券在握而失衡。
lines.detailEn: ‘Cutting through the amaranth on the high ground. Resolute, resolute. Walking in the middle brings no blame.’ The fifth line is the ruler of the hexagram. The remaining negative element is like a weed that can be cleared, but only by acting from the center. Leadership here requires both decisiveness and balance; excess would spoil success.

lines.textZh: 上六：无号，终有凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: No cry. In the end misfortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 没有号令警示，最终有凶险。阴柔居顶，孤立无援。
lines.interpretationEn: No cry of warning. In the end, misfortune comes. The weak at the top stands alone.
lines.detailZh: ‘无号，终有凶。’上六为夬卦唯一阴爻，居穷极之地。若到了最后还不警醒、不呼号求变，终必致凶。此爻既指被清除的阴邪，也提醒任何人在局势末端若拒绝面对现实，便会错失最后的转机。
lines.detailEn: ‘No cry. In the end, misfortune.’ The top line is the sole yin line, isolated at the end of the process. If there is still no alarm, no repentance, and no response to reality, misfortune becomes inevitable. It represents both the remnant to be removed and the danger of denial at the final stage.

overviewZh: 夬卦为《易经》第四十三卦，上兑下乾，象为‘泽上于天’。泽气上腾，水势将决，故名‘夬’，有决断、决裂、突破之义。此卦所表现的，不是鲁莽的冲锋，而是在阳气盛极、阴邪仅存之时，以公开、果断而合乎中道的方式清除隐患。卦中五阳决一阴，显示正道已占优势，但最后的一点阴柔若处理失当，仍可能酿成后患。因此夬卦强调：行动必须建立在诚信、号令、警惕与节制之上。它既是突破旧局的时刻，也是最考验德行与分寸的关口。若只知强行决裂，容易陷入激烈对抗；若能先明告众人、端正立场、施德于下，则能在危中取胜，实现真正的更新与前行。
overviewEn: Hexagram 43, Guai, is composed of Dui above and Qian below, often translated as Breakthrough or Resoluteness. Its image is ‘the lake rising above heaven,’ suggesting pressure building toward a decisive release. The hexagram describes a moment when what is right has gained clear strength, yet a remaining negative element must still be dealt with carefully. Five yang lines pressing out one yin line symbolize the need to remove corruption, weakness, or lingering disorder. However, Guai does not endorse reckless aggression. Its power lies in open declaration, moral clarity, and disciplined resolve. One must announce the issue publicly, act with sincerity, and remain alert to danger. The time calls for firmness without brutality, movement without impulsiveness, and courage balanced by self-command. Guai therefore represents the difficult threshold between tension and renewal: success comes not merely from strength, but from using strength ethically, visibly, and at the proper moment.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：‘扬于王庭，孚号，有厉，告自邑，不利即戎，利有攸往。’所谓‘扬于王庭’，是说必须把问题公开于公正权威之前，不可暗中私斗；‘孚号’则强调以诚信发号施令，使人心归附。夬卦虽主决断，但《彖传》特别指出：‘夬，决也，刚决柔也。健而说，决而和。扬于王庭，柔乘五刚也。孚号有厉，其危乃光也。不利即戎，所尚乃穷也。利有攸往，刚长乃终也。’其义在于，以刚健为体，以和悦为用，虽决却不失和。真正的突破，不靠一味斗争，而靠正义公开、上下同心。‘有厉’提醒人即使正义在我，也不能轻敌；‘不利即戎’说明轻启兵戈往往使事情走向极端。唯有在德义上站稳脚跟，既敢除弊，又知节制，方能使阳刚之道发展到最终的完成。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: ‘Proclaim it in the royal court. Cry out with sincerity. There is danger. Notify your own city. It is not favorable to resort to arms. It is favorable to have somewhere to go.’ Guai is about decisive action, yet the action must be public, justified, and morally grounded. The Tuan Commentary explains: ‘Guai means decision; the firm decisively removes the yielding. Strong and joyful, decisive yet harmonious. Proclaimed at the royal court, because the yielding rides upon five firm lines. Sincere alarm with danger—through awareness of danger, the light becomes manifest. It is not favorable to take up arms, because what is exalted in that way comes to exhaustion. Favorable to have somewhere to go, because the firm grows and reaches completion.’ This commentary is crucial: it teaches that true breakthrough is not mere force. The superior side wins because it combines strength with openness and firmness with harmony. Danger is not denied but acknowledged. Violence is discouraged because naked confrontation can consume even a righteous cause. Progress becomes possible when resolve is transparent, disciplined, and aligned with the growth of what is truly strong and correct.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：‘泽上于天，夬；君子以施禄及下，居德则忌。’泽本在天之下，今却上于天，形成水势欲决之象，故为夬。此象说明积聚到极点的力量终须疏导，不可久压。君子观此，不是借机炫耀权威，而是‘施禄及下’，把恩泽、资源、利益分施于下层，使人心安定、上下通达。‘居德则忌’则提醒：即使居功有德，也不可自满自恃。夬卦的危险，常不在外敌，而在胜势中的骄矜与刚愎。若能在突破之时仍保持谦抑，化决裂为治理，化清除为安民，则夬的刚健才真正转化为长久秩序。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: ‘The lake has risen above heaven: this is Guai. Thus the noble one dispenses riches downward and remains wary even while dwelling in virtue.’ Normally the lake is below the sky; here it is imagined as pressing above it, creating the sense of accumulated force nearing a break. The image suggests that tension cannot be suppressed forever and must be properly released. Yet the noble response is not self-display or domination. Instead, the superior person distributes benefits downward, sharing resources and stabilizing the people below. The second part is even more subtle: even one who stands in virtue must remain cautious. The danger of Guai often arises not from the opposing force alone, but from pride after gaining advantage. Thus the image teaches that decisive moments must be followed by generosity, humility, and good governance. Breakthrough is completed not when opposition is crushed, but when order is restored without arrogance.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代应用中，夬卦适合解释那些必须果断处理、但又不能失去规则感的局面。事业方面，它提示你面对团队隐患、低效合作、组织惰性或长期拖延的问题时，必须公开标准、明确边界，及时决断；但切忌情绪化开战，最好以制度、流程与共识推进改革。感情方面，夬卦多主关系进入摊牌期：不适合继续含糊、回避与消耗，应坦诚表达真实立场；若关系仍有基础，可借清晰沟通实现重建，若已失衡，则宜体面止损。财运方面，此卦利于清理烂账、斩断高风险项目、处理债务与非理性投入，强调审慎止损而非激进投机。健康方面，则提醒对积压已久的小病、炎症、压力和情绪问题不可再拖，越早检查、越早干预越好。总体而言，夬卦不是‘狠狠干一场’，而是以明快的判断、公开的原则与克制的行动，为人生打开新的通道。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Guai applies to situations that require a firm decision without losing ethical restraint. In career matters, it points to the need to confront inefficiency, hidden conflict, weak accountability, or toxic patterns in a team or organization. Standards should be stated clearly and action taken openly, but reform should proceed through principle and structure rather than emotional warfare. In relationships, Guai often signals a moment of truth: ambiguity can no longer be sustained. Honest communication becomes necessary, whether to rebuild trust on clearer terms or to end an unhealthy bond with dignity. In finance, the hexagram favors cutting losses, cleaning up debt, ending unsound investments, and withdrawing from risky speculation. It supports sober correction rather than aggressive gambling. In health, Guai warns against postponing treatment for chronic stress, inflammation, recurring symptoms, or emotional burden. Small issues become dangerous when ignored too long. Overall, this hexagram advises a disciplined breakthrough—clear judgment, direct speech, and measured action that opens a healthier path forward.
historicalStoryZh: 夬卦常被联想到周人伐纣之前的政治气象。商纣失德，朝廷上下虽多有不满，却长期隐忍未发；待天命、人心与诸侯向周汇聚之时，形势已如五阳决一阴，只剩最后的败德中心仍居高位。周武王并非轻率兴兵，而是先会盟诸侯，昭告天下，以正义名分统一共识，这正合‘扬于王庭，孚号’之义。即便如此，经典仍提醒‘有厉’与‘不利即戎’，因为再正当的决断，若失于躁进，也会招致巨大代价。故夬卦借此类历史经验说明：清除旧弊须先正名分、聚人心、明赏罚，再以果断之举完成转折。真正的突破从来不是一时血气，而是大势、德义与行动成熟后的临门一决。
historicalStoryEn: Guai is often associated with the political atmosphere before the Zhou conquest of Shang. King Zhou of Shang had lost virtue, and dissatisfaction had spread widely, yet for a time it remained unspoken or fragmented. When Heaven’s mandate, public sentiment, and the allegiance of the regional lords gradually turned toward Zhou, the situation resembled five yang lines pressing out one remaining yin line: the corrupt center still occupied power, but its foundation was failing. King Wu of Zhou did not act through blind impulse. He first gathered allies, clarified legitimacy, and made the cause publicly known—an echo of ‘proclaim it in the royal court’ and ‘cry out with sincerity.’ Even then, the text’s warning about danger and the unprofitability of arms remains relevant: righteous action can still become costly if driven by haste. The lesson is that true political breakthrough requires moral legitimacy, shared conviction, and disciplined timing before decisive action is taken.
relatedHexagramsNote: 夬与姤卦互为往来。夬是五阳决一阴，重在清除残余之弊；姤则是一阴初生，重在防微杜渐。夬之后若失警觉，旧患虽去，新阴又生，故突破之后更需守正与节制。

judgmentEn: Coming to Meet. The maiden is powerful. One should not marry such a maiden.
imageZh: 天下有风，姤；后以施命诰四方。
imageEn: Under heaven, wind: the image of Coming to Meet. Thus does the prince act when disseminating his commands and proclaiming them to the four quarters of heaven.
interpretationZh: 姤卦象征不期而遇，天下有风，无所不至。一阴初生于五阳之下，阴柔势力渐长，宜警惕防范。
interpretationEn: Gou symbolizes unexpected encounter. Wind under heaven reaches everywhere. One yin arises beneath five yang lines — the dark force grows. Be vigilant.

lines.textZh: 初六：系于金柅，贞吉，有攸往，见凶，羸豕孚蹢躅。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: It must be checked with a brake of bronze. Perseverance brings good fortune. If one lets it take its course, one experiences misfortune. Even a lean pig has it in him to rage around.
lines.interpretationZh: 用金属制动器拴住，坚守正道则吉。若放任则见凶，瘦弱的猪也会躁动不安。
lines.interpretationEn: Check it with a bronze brake. Perseverance brings fortune. Left unchecked, even a lean pig rages about. Misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 初六为一阴初生，力量尚微，却最具潜入之势。‘系于金柅，贞吉’是说必须在问题刚起时就加以约束，如用坚固车闩止车前行，守正则吉。若轻率前往，则见凶；‘羸豕孚蹢躅’形容看似瘦弱之猪，实则躁动欲奔，喻示小患不可轻视。此爻告诫人，对诱因、漏洞、越界关系或不良苗头，要在最初阶段立规矩、设边界，越早处理越易化险。
lines.detailEn: The first line shows yin just beginning to arise. Its visible strength is slight, yet its ability to slip inward is greatest. ‘Fasten it with a metal brake. Perseverance brings good fortune’ means that one must restrain the matter at its very beginning, as a strong wheel-block stops motion before it gathers force. If one pushes ahead carelessly, misfortune appears. The image of ‘a lean pig sincerely struggling and stamping’ suggests that what seems weak may in fact be restless and hard to control. This line warns against ignoring early temptations, loopholes, boundary violations, or unhealthy patterns. Set limits at once; the earlier one acts, the easier it is to avoid trouble.

lines.textZh: 九二：包有鱼，无咎，不利宾。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: There is a fish in the tank. No blame. It does not further guests.
lines.interpretationZh: 厨房中有鱼，无过失，但不宜招待宾客。
lines.interpretationEn: A fish in the tank. No blame. But it does not further one to entertain guests.
lines.detailZh: 九二居中得正，能稳住相遇之势。‘包有鱼，无咎，不利宾’中的鱼，象征所得之机与可控之物；包而有之，表示将机会收摄于内，不使外流，则可无咎。‘不利宾’则提醒不宜过度张扬或分散于外，不适合以此招待旁人、扩张场面。此爻之义在于：面对资源、信息或关系机会，应先内部消化、审慎掌握，低调处理比四处宣扬更有利。
lines.detailEn: The second line is firm, central, and properly placed, able to stabilize the encounter. In ‘There is a fish in the wrapping. No blame. It is not favorable for guests,’ the fish symbolizes a useful gain or manageable opportunity. To keep it wrapped means holding the matter inward and under control rather than letting it spill outward. ‘Not favorable for guests’ warns against display, diffusion, or premature sharing. The teaching is that when one acquires a resource, piece of information, or strategic relationship, it is better to consolidate it quietly and manage it carefully than to advertise it broadly or build outward too soon.

lines.textZh: 九三：臀无肤，其行次且，厉，无大咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If one is mindful of the danger, no great mistake is made.
lines.interpretationZh: 臀部没有皮肤，行走艰难，有危险，但无大过失。
lines.interpretationEn: No skin on the thighs; walking is difficult. Danger, but no great blame if one remains mindful.
lines.detailZh: 九三过刚而处下卦之上，进退失据。‘臀无肤，其行次且’写出行动艰难、坐立不安之象，仿佛行走时皮肤磨破，进又痛、停也难。此爻表示人在相遇之后已受牵扯，内心不安，处境危险，但‘无大咎’，说明虽有失措，尚未酿成大祸。关键在于及时止损，不可逞强前进。它常对应现实中关系暧昧、合作别扭、进场时机不佳等状态，宜忍痛调整。
lines.detailEn: The third line is overly forceful and stands at the top of the lower trigram, caught in an awkward position. ‘The buttocks have no skin; his walking is halting and difficult’ portrays discomfort, abrasion, and painful movement—unable to advance smoothly, yet also unable to rest at ease. This line suggests that after the encounter, one has already become entangled and now feels uneasy, exposed, and at risk. Still, ‘no great blame’ means the situation, though difficult, has not become irreparable. The crucial response is to cut losses in time rather than forcing progress. In modern terms, it often reflects strained cooperation, unhealthy ambiguity, or poor timing in entering a situation.

lines.textZh: 九四：包无鱼，起凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: No fish in the tank. This leads to misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 厨房中没有鱼，由此引发凶险。失去了应有的联系。
lines.interpretationEn: No fish in the tank. This gives rise to misfortune. The connection is lost.
lines.detailZh: 九四曰‘包无鱼，起凶’，说明本应掌握之机已经流失，空有其器而无其实。一旦在错误判断下再起动作，就容易招致凶祸。此爻多指上位者失察，未能把握基层动向，或在资源已失、时机已过的情况下仍强行操作，结果适得其反。姤卦到此，提醒人不是所有相遇都还能补救；若核心内容已失，最忌讳的是虚张声势、仓促发难。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says, ‘The wrapping contains no fish. Rising brings misfortune.’ What should have been secured is already gone; the container remains, but the substance is absent. If one now acts on mistaken assumptions, trouble follows. This line often points to leaders who have lost touch with what is actually happening below, or to efforts made after resources, timing, or substance have already been lost. At this stage, Gou teaches that not every missed encounter can be recovered. When the essential content is gone, the worst response is bluster, forced action, or hasty escalation.

lines.textZh: 九五：以杞包瓜，含章，有陨自天。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: A melon covered with willow leaves. Hidden lines. Then it drops down to one from heaven.
lines.interpretationZh: 用杞柳包裹甜瓜，内含文采，有从天而降的好运。
lines.interpretationEn: A melon wrapped in willow leaves. Hidden brilliance within. Something drops from heaven.
lines.detailZh: 九五中正尊位，是全卦最能善用‘遇’的一爻。‘以杞包瓜，含章，有陨自天’意象丰富：以杞叶包瓜，既保护成果，又不炫露；‘含章’指内藏文采德性，不轻示人；‘有陨自天’则象征意外之福、自上而来的成就。此爻说明，真正高明的掌控不是压制一切，而是以柔护成、以内养外，在不张扬中自然得助。用于现实，就是有格局者能容纳新机缘，同时守住原则，于是好运反而从天而降。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line, central and correct in the ruler’s place, is the most capable of using encounter well. ‘Wrap the melon with willow leaves. Contain the brilliance. There is something falling from heaven.’ The imagery is layered: wrapping the melon protects what is valuable without exposing it; ‘containing brilliance’ means holding virtue and capability inward rather than displaying them prematurely; ‘falling from heaven’ suggests unexpected blessing or support from above. This line teaches that true mastery does not mean suppressing everything. It means protecting what is growing, nurturing substance inwardly, and remaining modest. In practical life, those with real stature can receive new opportunities without losing principle, and help or success arrives almost naturally.

lines.textZh: 上九：姤其角，吝，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: He comes to meet with his horns. Humiliation. But no blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用角去迎接，虽有困窘，但无过失。刚强过度，不善交际。
lines.interpretationEn: Coming to meet with horns. Humiliation, but no blame. Excessive rigidity in social encounters.
lines.detailZh: 上九曰‘姤其角，吝，无咎。’角在最上，象征相遇已到极端，彼此接触带着硬碰硬的意味。‘吝’表示局面尴尬、收束不美，却‘无咎’，因为守住了最后的边界，没有进一步失德失位。此爻提醒，当事情已发展到难以圆融时，与其继续纠缠，不如明确拒止，哪怕显得生硬，也胜过继续失控。对人际、合作、利益冲突而言，这是一种迟来的止界：虽不圆满，但及时刹车，仍可免于更大过失。
lines.detailEn: The top line says, ‘Coming to meet at the horns. Regret, but no blame.’ Horns at the very top suggest encounter taken to an extreme, where contact has become hard, confrontational, and difficult to soften. ‘Regret’ indicates awkwardness and an unsatisfactory ending, yet ‘no blame’ shows that a final boundary was still preserved. This line teaches that when matters have advanced too far for graceful harmony, a firm refusal may be better than continued entanglement. It may look harsh or inelegant, but it prevents deeper loss. In relationships, partnerships, or conflicts of interest, this is the late but necessary act of drawing a line.

overviewZh: 姤卦，上乾下巽，风行天下，象征突然而来的相遇、接触与牵引。‘姤’不是普通的相逢，而是强势因素骤然进入既有秩序，引发注意、诱惑、警觉与调整。卦辞言‘女壮，勿用取女’，并非单指婚嫁，而是提醒人在面对来势迅猛、外柔内强之事时，不可因一时吸引而仓促接纳。此卦核心在于辨别：什么是短暂机缘，什么是潜在失衡。若能守中持正，明辨边界，把握分寸，则相遇可成为契机；若失于防微杜渐，让小势坐大，便可能由邂逅演成牵制。姤卦因此强调领导者与行动者都要在变化初起时作出判断，以清醒节制回应外来的力量。
overviewEn: Hexagram 44, Gou, consists of Qian above and Xun below: wind under heaven. It describes an unexpected encounter, the sudden arrival of a force that enters an established order and demands attention. This is not merely a pleasant meeting, but a contact charged with attraction, influence, and possible imbalance. The Judgment warns, ‘The woman is powerful; do not marry such a woman,’ which in a broader sense cautions against hastily embracing something that appears alluring yet may overturn proper balance. The heart of the hexagram is discernment: to distinguish a passing opportunity from a disruptive influence. If one remains centered, principled, and clear about boundaries, the encounter can become useful and transformative. If one indulges impulse and lets a minor influence grow unchecked, the result may be entanglement and loss of control. Gou therefore teaches timely recognition, self-command, and wise response at the very beginning of change.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：‘姤：女壮，勿用取女。’《彖传》进一步解释：‘姤，遇也，柔遇刚也。勿用取女，不可与长也。天地相遇，品物咸章也；刚遇中正，天下大行也。姤之时义大矣哉。’其义在于，一阴初生而上遇五阳，所遇虽小，却有极强渗透力。所谓‘不可与长’，不是一概否定相遇，而是告诫人不可让不当之势长期坐大。若把这层意思用于处世，则是对欲望、风险、关系、舆论乃至外来机会的预警：凡突然出现而极具吸引力者，尤其需要审其根基与后势。与此同时，彖传也指出天地相遇能使万物彰明，说明‘遇’本身并非凶，关键在于是否合于中正。合道则通行天下，失道则反受其困。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, ‘Coming to Meet. The woman is powerful. Do not marry such a woman.’ The Tuan Commentary explains: ‘Gou means encounter; the yielding meets the firm. Do not marry such a woman, because she cannot be allowed to grow. When heaven and earth meet, all beings flourish and become manifest. When the firm meets what is central and correct, the way prevails under heaven. Great indeed is the meaning of the time of Gou.’ The key idea is that a single yin line arises below and meets five yang lines above. What first appears small may possess strong power of penetration. ‘Cannot be allowed to grow’ does not condemn all encounters; rather, it warns against letting an improper influence become established. Applied to life, this speaks to desire, temptation, risky opportunities, persuasive people, public opinion, or any force that arrives suddenly and attracts quick attachment. Such things must be examined at their root and in their likely development. Yet the commentary also says that when heaven and earth meet, the myriad beings are revealed. Encounter itself is not evil. What matters is whether it accords with centrality and correctness. If aligned with principle, encounter brings expansion; if not, it becomes a source of entanglement.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：‘天下有风，姤；后以施命诰四方。’天上无所不覆，风行无所不入，故姤卦之象是讯息、影响与变化迅速传播。古代君后观此卦，不是被动等待风起，而是主动发布政令、申明纪律，使四方知所趋避。象义说明，当外部气机开始流动时，最重要的是先统一原则、厘清边界、明示方向。风若无节，则扰乱；风若有命，则通达。因此姤卦的形象并不只是‘相遇’，更是‘影响开始扩散’。人在此时应如执政者一般，及早发声、及时规范，把问题处理在萌芽，把机会导入正轨。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, ‘Under heaven there is wind: Coming to Meet. Thus the ruler issues commands and proclaims them to the four quarters.’ Heaven covers all things, and wind reaches everywhere; together they symbolize influence, messages, and change spreading quickly across the world. The ancient ruler, seeing this image, did not merely wait for events to unfold. Instead, they issued clear orders and announced standards so all directions would know what to follow and what to avoid. The image teaches that when currents begin to move, one must establish principles early, define boundaries, and communicate direction plainly. Wind without guidance becomes disturbance; wind guided by mandate becomes circulation and order. Thus Gou is not only about encounter but about the rapid diffusion of influence after contact occurs. In such a time, one should act like a wise leader: speak early, regulate promptly, and guide both risks and opportunities before they grow beyond control.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：姤卦提示职场中会出现突发合作、强势客户、热门项目或外来资源，看似难得，实则必须先辨真伪与代价。适合快速评估、谨慎接触，不宜被短期利益牵着走。感情：此卦常主强烈吸引、快速靠近、关系升温很快，但未必稳固。若彼此价值观、边界感不明，越热烈越要放慢节奏，避免因迷恋而忽略长期相处的结构性问题。财运：可能有突然的投资机会、市场风口或高回报诱因，宜设止损、控仓位、重合同，切忌因‘别人都在上’就盲目跟进。健康：多与内热外动、情绪波动、睡眠不稳、肝胆与循环系统压力有关，也提示应减少被外界刺激持续裹挟。总体而言，姤卦不是拒绝机会，而是要求人在机会来临之初就守住原则、节奏与主导权，让相遇成为可用之机，而非失控之端。
modernApplicationEn: Career: Gou suggests sudden partnerships, forceful clients, trendy projects, or outside resources entering your work. They may look promising, but must be examined for cost, motive, and long-term effect. This is a time for quick assessment and cautious engagement, not for being dragged by short-term gain. Relationships: the hexagram often points to strong attraction, rapid closeness, and fast emotional escalation, yet not necessarily stability. If shared values and boundaries are unclear, the more exciting the connection feels, the more important it is to slow down and look beneath the surface. Wealth: there may be abrupt investment opportunities, market hype, or high-return temptations. Risk control is essential—set limits, protect capital, read terms carefully, and do not follow the crowd just because momentum is strong. Health: this hexagram can reflect overstimulation, emotional agitation, restless sleep, and stress affecting circulation or liver-gallbladder balance in traditional symbolism. Overall, Gou does not teach avoidance of opportunity; it teaches maintaining principle, timing, and self-command from the very moment opportunity appears, so that an encounter becomes useful rather than destabilizing.
historicalStoryZh: 可借汉高祖初见韩信之事会通姤意。韩信早年未受重用，后经萧何月下追韩信，刘邦方得此大将。此为‘遇’之象：关键人物忽然进入局势，足以改写全局。但若只见其锋芒而无制度节制，也可能反受其制。刘邦后来既重用韩信，又不断通过封赏、分权与政治安排加以节衡，正体现姤卦所示‘遇而不可失其主’。相遇本身成就大业，如何安顿相遇之后的权力与边界，才决定吉凶。姤卦借此提醒：真正重要的，不只是遇见谁，而是能否在遇见之后仍守住秩序与主动。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical parallel is Liu Bang’s eventual recognition of Han Xin in the early Han dynasty. Han Xin was initially overlooked, until Xiao He famously pursued him and brought him back, allowing Liu Bang to recognize an extraordinary military talent. This is very much the image of Gou: a crucial figure suddenly enters the field and has the power to change the whole situation. Yet if one sees only brilliance and not the need for structure, such a force may later become difficult to manage. Liu Bang both employed Han Xin and sought to balance power through titles, distribution of authority, and political restraint. That reflects the teaching of Gou: one may meet what is powerful, but must not lose command of the larger order. The encounter can help accomplish great things, yet the outcome depends on whether boundaries, role, and authority remain clear after the meeting.
relatedHexagramsNote: 姤与夬互有深意：夬是决断已成，姤是外势初遇；夬重去小人，姤重防微杜渐。又可参观遁卦，遁言退避其势，姤言先识其机。三卦合看，更能理解面对强势因素时，何时断、何时防、何时退。
references: 《象传》：天下有风，姤；后以施命诰四方
